## vvx
Job Jockey Kit - MCP tools for project initiative management

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) on a cron schedule or once at a specific time. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- rbk-prep-release: Prepare release candidate for upstream delivery
- vvc-BREAK-LOCK: Force-break a stuck VVC lock after a crash.
- vvc-commit: Guarded git commit
- cma-prep-pr: Prepare branch for upstream PR contribution
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

  ## Character
  
  Spec-only restructure of RBSRR-RegimeRepo.adoc to land BBAAQ Surface 4's
  depot-identity-collapse retirement at the regime spec itself. RBSRR is the
  spec that *defines* the rbrr.env regime — its references to the three
  retired RBRR fields are definition sites, not xrefs, so the work is
  structural rather than the mechanical xref-flipping that BBAAV closed out.
  Carved out of BBAAV at mount time when the scope contradiction surfaced
  (BBAAV's docket said "no behavioral or structural changes to spec content
  beyond xref flipping").
  
  Pure .adoc — no code, no narrative tracks. Runs in parallel with any
  non-spec work.
  
  ## Scope
  
  **RBSRR-RegimeRepo.adoc — delete `axhrv_variable` definition blocks for the
  three retired fields:**
  
  - `{rbrr_depot_project_id}` (lines 30-39 at slate time)
  - `{rbrr_gar_repository}` (lines 52-59 at slate time)
  - `{rbrr_gcb_pool_stem}` (lines 163-170 at slate time)
  
  **RBSRR-RegimeRepo.adoc — add `axhrv_variable` definition block for
  `{rbrr_depot_moniker}`:**
  
  The new field is the second half of the depot identity tuple (with
  `RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX`). Place it in the Resource Prefixing group alongside
  `{rbrr_cloud_prefix}` so the tuple lives together. Description: format
  `^[a-z][a-z0-9]*$`, length 1–26; combines with `RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX` to
  derive depot project ID, GAR repository, pool stem, and bucket via RBDC.
  
  **RBSRR-RegimeRepo.adoc — update intro prose:**
  
  Line 8 currently mentions "depot project identifiers, repository locations"
  as configured by RBRR. Update to reflect that depot identity is now
  `(CLOUD_PREFIX, DEPOT_MONIKER)` and the four derived names fall out as RBDC
  constants.
  
  **RBS0-SpecTop.adoc — add mapping entry:**
  
  `:rbrr_depot_moniker: <<rbrr_depot_moniker,RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER>>` alongside
  the other rbrr_* mappings around line 326 (next to `rbrr_cloud_prefix`).
  
  **RBSGM-governor_mantle.adoc — line 27 stale pattern (surfaced during
  BBAAV):**
  
  The check `«DEPOT_PROJECT_ID»` matches pattern `rbw-*-[0-9]{12}` references
  the OLD timestamped project-ID shape. Under the new
  `${CLOUD_PREFIX}d-${MONIKER}` shape, the regex no longer matches. Decide:
  fix in this pace (small, local) or itch.

  - AsciiDoc rendering of RBSRR shows no literal `{...}` strings and the new

  - Code changes to rbrr_regime.sh (already landed under BBAAM).

  - Sweeping other specs for stale `rbw-*-[0-9]{12}`-shape literals (capture
    as itch if found beyond RBSGM:27).
  - Tabtargets, CLAUDE.md acronym map, code modules.
  
  ## References
  
  - BBAAV — companion mechanical xref sweep that this carves out from.
  - BBAAM — depot-identity-collapse code landing (commits ee816373 →
    d2dec554).
  - BBAAQ commit ledger — Surface 4 noted RBSRR in its 11-spec list but did
    not rewrite definition sites.
  - RBSDE-depot_levy.adoc — Surface 4 precedent for derivation prose
    (literal `${RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX}` env-var form, `(resolved by RBDC_*)`
    parenthetical).

Root cause: openssl enc -base64 -d silently produces empty output (exit 0) when input is single-line base64 without PEM-style ~76-char line breaks. Documented OpenSSL behavior across all variants tested (https://wiki.openssl.org/index.php/Base64): 'If there is no newline on decode, it silently returns no data, which can be confusing; ... on commandline enc specify -A'. Empirically verified across three platforms / three OpenSSL variants: macOS LibreSSL 3.3.6 (system /usr/bin/openssl), macOS OpenSSL 3.5.2 (Homebrew /opt/homebrew/bin/openssl), Linux Ubuntu OpenSSL 3.0.13 (cerebro /usr/bin/openssl) — all three produce 0 bytes WITHOUT -A and full correct output WITH -A for single-line input. -A flag is documented in OpenSSL master docs, 1.1.1 docs, AND OpenBSD man pages (LibreSSL); cross-implementation stable since at least OpenSSL 1.0.0c. Why the bug went latent until now: pre-AUAAr (commit 385589f4, April 1, 2026), the code used 'base64 -d' (GNU) with 'base64 -D' (BSD) fallback — both GNU and BSD base64 silently tolerate long single-line input, so mantle worked everywhere. Pristine-lifecycle case 3 (rbtdrp_governor_lifecycle) advanced past the BBAAS over-gate (Cycle 2) and the missing buf_fact source (Cycle 3), through 11 of 12 mantle steps successfully (depot validation, SA list, governor SA creation, IAM owner grant, keys.create with 200), then died at 'Convert JSON key to RBRA format' with 'Empty client_email in key JSON' — because the decoded key file rbgp_governor_key.json was 0 bytes despite a 3192-byte privateKeyData arriving from Google's keys.create endpoint. Root cause: openssl enc -base64 -d silently produces empty output (exit 0) when input is single-line base64 without PEM-style ~76-char line breaks. Documented OpenSSL behavior across all variants tested (https://wiki.openssl.org/index.php/Base64): 'If there is no newline on decode, it silently returns no data, which can be confusing; ... on commandline enc specify -A'. Empirically verified across three platforms / three OpenSSL variants: macOS LibreSSL 3.3.6 (system /usr/bin/openssl), macOS OpenSSL 3.5.2 (Homebrew /opt/homebrew/bin/openssl), Linux Ubuntu OpenSSL 3.0.13 (cerebro /usr/bin/openssl) — all three produce 0 bytes WITHOUT -A and full correct output WITH -A for single-line input. -A flag is documented in OpenSSL master docs, 1.1.1 docs, AND OpenBSD man pages (LibreSSL); cross-implementation stable since at least OpenSSL 1.0.0c. Why the bug went latent until now: pre-AUAAr (commit 385589f4, April 1, 2026), the code used 'base64 -d' (GNU) with 'base64 -D' (BSD) fallback — both GNU and BSD base64 silently tolerate long single-line input, so mantle worked everywhere. 1	# jjezs_paddock ₣BB
2	
3	## Context
4	
5	Release qualification gap surfaced by the ₣A_ depot-regen heat. Every existing test tier (`rbw-tf` fast, `rbw-tr` release) tolerates accumulated depot state, so silent first-build assumptions slip through. Recent spooks — kludge-aware-charge-prereq (k-prefixed hallmarks at GAR), ZRBOB_PROJECT (compose project name without runtime prefix), rbob_charged_predicate prefix omission, reliquary integrity-broken on negative test — all share a property: they only manifest on first-build paths, and none were caught until live verification surfaced them.
6	
7	This heat constructs `rbw-tP` QualifyPristine — a third tier that **refuses to run** unless marshal-zero state was just committed. The refusal is enforced by the test itself, not by ceremony or operator discipline.
8	
9	## Single operator prerequisite chain
10	
11	Operator has exactly one prerequisite chain: confirm Payor health → marshal-zero → commit → run `rbw-tP`. Payor OAuth is the only credential the operator must have ready; everything else (governor, retriever, director SAs and their RBRA files) is **minted by the qualification itself**, not restored from backup.
12	
13	## Entry contract — load-bearing
14	
15	`rbw-tP` fails-fast unless ALL of the following hold:
16	
17	- Working tree clean
18	- HEAD commit is a marshal-zero commit (detectable signature baked into `rblm_zero` per BBAAB)
19	- RBRR fields are blank (prefixes, depot project ID, etc.)
20	- No RBRA credential files present (governor, retriever, director — all deleted by marshal-zero, recreated by the qualification)
21	- No hallmark fields populated in nameplates
22	- No depot-scoped fields populated in vessel rbrv.env files
23	
24	Operator cannot skip the prerequisite. This is the property that makes the tier catch the silent-first-build bug class by construction.
25	
26	## Failure mode contract
27	
28	Mid-qualification failure means start-over-from-zero, not patch-and-continue. Documented explicitly in runbook to prevent the very accumulated-state bug class this tier exists to catch.
29	
30	## Release-branch execution contract
31	
32	Three properties bind every fixture and phase in this tier.
33	
34	**Run on a release branch.** The release machinery is engineered for branch execution, not main. The branch will accumulate many commits across a successful pristine run. After a failed run, the branch holds bygone commits that `rbw-MZ` does not touch — they remain as history; the next run starts from a fresh marshal-zero commit on top.
35	
36	**Commits during the run are first-class.** Each step that mutates regime state (rbrr.env, rbra files, vessel rbrv.env, etc.) commits the change. Container-recipe work has a documented cognitive failure mode: humans lose track of where they are in long sequences. The growing commit trail is the operator's mental anchor against that.
37	
38	**Stop on very first failure, cleanly and clearly.** A step fails → fixture stops → operator stops → debugging happens immediately on the failed branch. Stderr names the failed step; fixture exits non-zero. Recovery is `rbw-MZ` + retry on a fresh marshal-zero. No graceful degradation, no recovery branches.
39	
40	Engineering scaffolding for any other shape — partial-run cleanup, soft-delete tolerance, multi-mode failure handling, recovery-time orphan inspection, diagnostic-redundancy — is non-load-bearing and does not belong in pristine-tier code. The single mechanical path is the entire surface.
41	
42	## Tier layering
43	
44	Three tiers, escalating cost:
45	
46	| Colophon | Frontispiece | Cost | Entry contract |
47	|----------|--------------|------|----------------|
48	| `rbw-tf` | QualifyFast | seconds | none |
49	| `rbw-tr` | QualifyRelease | minutes | none (accumulated state OK) |
50	| `rbw-tP` | QualifyPristine *(new)* | ~1 hour + cloud $ | marshal-zero just committed |
51	
52	`rbw-tP` is THE release gate. `rbw-tr` becomes the pre-pristine smoke test — cheap-and-frequent for development confidence; pristine for actual release.
53	
54	## Operator scope
55	
56	Single operator (project lead). Not designed for multi-operator workflow. Runbook lives in README.md release section.
57	
58	## Coupling to ₣A_
59	
60	Runs concurrently with ₣A_'s remaining paces; ₣A_'s one-time cutover informs `rbw-tP`'s sequence.
61	
62	## Depot identity collapse — locked design decisions (₢BBAAM)
63	
64	These decisions emerged in conversation during ₢BBAAM Step 1. They persist for the rest of the pace and constrain BBAAK cases 4-5 (the follow-on pace).
65	
66	**Maximal collapse.** RBRR drops `RBRR_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID`, `RBRR_GAR_REPOSITORY`, `RBRR_GCB_POOL_STEM` and gains `RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER` (format `^[a-z][a-z0-9]*$`, length 1–26). Project ID, GAR repo, pool stem, and bucket fall out as RBDC kindle constants from `(RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX, RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER)`. The minimal-collapse alternative (drop only the timestamp, keep the three RBRR fields) was considered and rejected — it would leave the operator hand-pasting derivable values into RBRR after every levy.
67	
68	**CLOUD_PREFIX scope.** `RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX` flows uniformly through depot project_id, GAR repo, pool stem, AND bucket (depot-affiliated resources). Payor remains on `RBGC_GLOBAL_PREFIX` (installation-scoped, not depot-affiliated); payor naming uniformity is out of scope for this pace.
69	
70	**Naming shapes.** Type markers and pool suffixes live as literal strings inside RBDC, keeping rbdc kindle independent of rbgc kindle ordering:
71	
72	- Project: `${CLOUD_PREFIX}d-${MONIKER}`
73	- GAR repository: `${CLOUD_PREFIX}${MONIKER}-gar` (note: `-gar` suffix, not `-repository`)
74	- Pool stem: `${CLOUD_PREFIX}${MONIKER}-pool`
75	- Bucket: `${CLOUD_PREFIX}b-${MONIKER}`
76	
77	**409 posture deferred.** Timestamp drop alone creates the operator-visible "fails if exists" property: re-levying a moniker collides at project-create against the GCP soft-delete graveyard. Downstream sub-resource 409 posture is left as today — bucket fatal; AR / pools idempotent. A pre-locked redesign here would over-engineer; surface fixture-driven need before reshaping.
78	
79	**Mason SA name unchanged.** `mason-${moniker}` retained; broader SA-naming uniformity deferred.
80	
81	**Pristine-fixture moniker autodetect lives in Rust, not payor.** Theurge-side autodetect for pristine-lifecycle cases 2-3 — family stems (e.g. `pristq`, `pristg`) and the `100000` floor — lives in `rbtdrp_pristine.rs`. Payor `rbgp_depot_list` emits per-moniker fact files; theurge consumes them and computes the next free six-digit numeric increment per family prefix. Payor stays uncoupled from fixture identity space.
82	
83	**Depot fact-file shape.** Per-moniker fact files use `<moniker>.${RBGP_FACT_EXT_DEPOT}` (extension constant resolves to `depot`). File content is one of `RBGP_DEPOT_STATE_COMPLETE`, `RBGP_DEPOT_STATE_BROKEN`, `RBGP_DEPOT_STATE_DELETE_REQUESTED`. State and extension tinder constants live in `rbgc_Constants.sh` (added in Step 1).
84	
85	## Cult-verb naming decision — domain-exclusive split
86	
87	JJ owns "muster" as a verb (`jjx_list`). Recipe Bottle's two unrelated muster uses both rename to avoid the collision. The two replacement verbs are **domain-exclusive** — each belongs to one domain only and does not substitute for the other.
88	
89	**SA cult-verb (BBAAN surface):** muster → **roster**. Colophons `rbw-arr` (retriever), `rbw-adr` (director). Constants `RBYC_ROSTER`, `RBZ_ROSTER_RETRIEVER`, `RBZ_ROSTER_DIRECTOR`. Functions `rbgg_roster_retrievers`, `rbgg_roster_directors`. Verb-as-noun ceremony: `(Roster):`.
90	
91	**Image domain (landed code, separate rename pace):** muster → **audit**. Colophons `rbw-iah/iar/iae`. Constants `RBZ_AUDIT_HALLMARKS/RELIQUARIES/ENSHRINEMENTS` and Rust `RBTDRM_COLOPHON_AUDIT_*`. Functions `rbfl_audit_hallmarks/reliquaries/enshrinements`. Spec quoin `:rbtgo_image_audit:`; spec file likely renames RBSIM → RBSIA (BBAAQ decides).
92	
93	**Case discipline.** Lowercase tail letter on both new colophons preserves BBAAN's existing rule: capitals (`I`, `D`) for cloud-mutating verbs; lowercase (`r`, `a`) for no-cloud-change observation. Mantle (`rbw-aM`) unchanged.
94	
95	**Cross-pace ordering.** BBAAQ (spec sweep) lands the spec-side rename first, so spec is source-of-truth. The image-rename code pace executes after BBAAQ. BBAAN's docket has been updated to use roster vocabulary directly. BBAAC and BBAAG are touched only at colophon/verb references. BBAAR's docket is already abstract ("adapt to whatever BBAAN settles") and inherits the new vocabulary without redocketing.
96	
97	## References

99	- ₣A_ rbk-mvp-3-resource-prefix-and-depot-regen — surfaced the gap
100	- `tt/rbw-tr.QualifyRelease.sh` — current release qualify; layered alongside, not replaced

102	- `.claude/commands/rbk-prep-release.md` — upstream contribution ceremony; pristine-pass becomes a precondition (BBAAH)

## Context

**Heat ₣BB** rbk-mvp-3-release-qualification (racing, 9/21). Paddock builds `rbw-tP` QualifyPristine — a third tier that refuses to run unless marshal-zero state was just committed. Carries locked design decisions from BBAAM's depot-identity-collapse (RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER replaces three retired fields; `(CLOUD_PREFIX, DEPOT_MONIKER)` tuple; derived names fall out as RBDC kindle constants).

**Recent work** (last 5): four BBAAM verification cycles landed follow-on fixes (rbte_cli over-gate, rbgp_cli kindle gate, rbgp missing buf_fact source, openssl `-A` flag for base64 decode), plus BBAAV's mechanical xref sweep across 7 sibling specs — which carved BBAAY out at mount time when scope contradiction surfaced.

**Pace ₢BBAAY** silks `spec-rbrr-regime-restructure` — fits the docket. No rename suggested.

## Docket summary

Pure .adoc, four .adoc surfaces:

1. **RBSRR delete** — three `axhrv_variable` blocks for retired fields (`rbrr_depot_project_id`, `rbrr_gar_repository`, `rbrr_gcb_pool_stem`)
2. **RBSRR add** — `axhrv_variable` block for `rbrr_depot_moniker` in Resource Prefixing group next to `rbrr_cloud_prefix`
3. **RBSRR intro prose** — line 8 reframe to tuple-based identity
4. **RBS0 mapping** — add `:rbrr_depot_moniker:` near line 326
5. **RBSGM:27 decision** — stale `rbw-*-[0-9]{12}` regex; fix-here-vs-itch judgment call

## Approach

- Read RBSRR-RegimeRepo.adoc + RBSDE-depot_levy.adoc precedent + RBS0 mapping section first to ground definition-block shape and placement
- Apply the four RBSRR edits + the RBS0 mapping in one pass
- Inspect RBSGM:27 in context, then call the fix-or-itch decision (short-look first, escalate to user if non-obvious)
- Run the three grep verifications + AsciiDoc render check
- Notch with `jjx_record` against ₢BBAAY

## Execution strategy

Sequential **sonnet** — single concentrated AsciiDoc surface, mechanical with one judgment call (RBSGM:27). No parallelization benefit; reads inform writes.

1	//axhrb_regime
2	{rbrr_regime}
3	
4	This regime specifies variables that define repository-wide configuration and installation-wide settings within the Recipe Bottle system.
5	Each
6	{rbrr_regime}
7	instance represents foundational infrastructure configuration that rarely changes and is shared across all Recipe Bottle operations.
8	RBRR assignment files are found in the operator's local filesystem (typically `../station-files/rbrr.env`) and configure depot project identifiers, regional settings, repository locations, and service account credential file paths.
9	
10	//axhrgb_group
11	{rbrr_group_resource_prefixing}
12	
13	Operator-chosen prefixes that scope resource names across the cloud and local-runtime namespaces.
14	Enables multi-regime isolation on shared infrastructure — distinct depots or developers can coexist in the same GCP project or on the same developer workstation without name collisions.
15	Both variables must be populated — the marshal-zero path blanks them so a fresh regime requires an explicit operator decision before `rbrr_validate` passes.
16	The prefix must be 2–11 characters, start with a lowercase letter, and end with a hyphen (regex `^[a-z][a-z0-9-]*-$`).

2	{rbrr_regime}
3	
4	This regime specifies variables that define repository-wide configuration and installation-wide settings within the Recipe Bottle system.
5	Each
6	{rbrr_regime}
7	instance represents foundational infrastructure configuration that rarely changes and is shared across all Recipe Bottle operations.
8	RBRR assignment files are found in the operator's local filesystem (typically `../station-files/rbrr.env`) and configure the depot identity tuple `(RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX, RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER)` (from which depot project ID, GAR repository, pool stem, and bucket fall out as RBDC constants), regional settings, and service account credential file paths.
9	
10	//axhrgb_group
11	{rbrr_group_resource_prefixing}
12	
13	Operator-chosen identifiers that scope resource names across the cloud and local-runtime namespaces.
14	Enables multi-regime isolation on shared infrastructure — distinct depots or developers can coexist in the same GCP project or on the same developer workstation without name collisions.
15	All three variables must be populated — the marshal-zero path blanks them so a fresh regime requires an explicit operator decision before `rbrr_validate` passes.
16	The two prefixes must be 2–11 characters, start with a lowercase letter, and end with a hyphen (regex `^[a-z][a-z0-9-]*-$`).

23	substitutions, and other GCP-scoped identifiers.

26	{rbrr_depot_moniker}
27	
28	Stem identifier that combines with
29	{rbrr_cloud_prefix}
30	to form the depot identity tuple.
31	Must be 1–26 characters, start with a lowercase letter, and contain only lowercase alphanumerics (regex `^[a-z][a-z0-9]*$`).
32	The tuple derives `RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID`, `RBDC_GAR_REPOSITORY`, `RBDC_GCB_POOL_STEM`, and `RBDC_GCS_BUCKET` at kindle time.

46	and

52	
53	Credential file paths derived from a single enrolled directory for each operational role.

55	{gcp_service}

58	{rbtr_payor}
59	operations.

Carved out of BBAAV at mount time when the scope contradiction surfaced — BBAAV's docket said 'no behavioral or structural changes to spec content beyond xref flipping', but RBSRR's references to the three retired RBRR fields are definition sites, not xrefs. RBSRR-RegimeRepo.adoc: deleted three axhrv_variable definition blocks for the retired fields ({rbrr_depot_project_id}, {rbrr_gar_repository}, {rbrr_gcb_pool_stem}); added one axhrgv_variable block for the new {rbrr_depot_moniker} field (placed in the Resource Prefixing group between {rbrr_cloud_prefix} and {rbrr_runtime_prefix} so the depot identity tuple lives together — followed file's group convention axhrgv over docket's literal axhrv since cloud_prefix uses axhrgv); updated group intro at lines 13-16 from 'Operator-chosen prefixes' / 'Both variables must be populated' / 'The prefix must be 2-11 characters' to 'Operator-chosen identifiers' / 'All three variables must be populated' / 'The two prefixes must be 2-11 characters' since the moniker has a different format (^[a-z][a-z0-9]*$, length 1-26) than the prefix format (^[a-z][a-z0-9-]*-$); updated intro prose at line 8 from 'depot project identifiers, regional settings, repository locations, and service account credential file paths' to 'depot identity tuple (RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX, RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER) (from which depot project ID, GAR repository, pool stem, and bucket fall out as RBDC constants), regional settings, and service account credential file paths' — phrasing 'fall out as RBDC constants' matches paddock vocabulary at BB. Variable description for depot_moniker grounded in RBSDE-depot_levy.adoc precedent (line 31): naming 'Stem identifier that combines with {rbrr_cloud_prefix} to form the depot identity tuple', followed by format constraint and 'The tuple derives RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID, RBDC_GAR_REPOSITORY, RBDC_GCB_POOL_STEM, and RBDC_GCS_BUCKET at kindle time'. RBS0-SpecTop.adoc: added :rbrr_depot_moniker: <<rbrr_depot_moniker,RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER>> mapping at line 327, between rbrr_cloud_prefix and rbrr_runtime_prefix to preserve adjacency. Confirmed retired RBRR attributes (rbrr_depot_project_id, rbrr_gar_repository, rbrr_gcb_pool_stem) already absent from RBS0 mappings — BBAAQ Surface 4 had previously scrubbed them. RBSGM-governor_mantle.adoc:27 fix-or-itch decision (docket explicit: 'Decide: fix in this pace (small, local) or itch'): fixed in this pace. The require step '«DEPOT_PROJECT_ID» matches pattern rbw-*-[0-9]{12}' references the OLD timestamped project-ID shape from before BBAAM's depot-identity-collapse — under the new ${CLOUD_PREFIX}d-${MONIKER} shape (where CLOUD_PREFIX is ^[a-z][a-z0-9-]*-$ and MONIKER is ^[a-z][a-z0-9]*$, length 1-26), the rbw-*-[0-9]{12} pattern can never match. Replaced lines 26-27 with a NOTE following RBSDE-depot_levy.adoc:38 precedent ('Joint length ... is enforced upstream by zrbrr_enforce at regime kindle time, so this step performs no length validation') — replacement reads 'NOTE: Format and joint length of ${RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX} + d- + ${RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER} are enforced upstream by zrbrr_enforce at regime kindle time, so this step performs no shape validation'. Choice rationale: deleting outright would remove documentation value; updating regex to new shape would re-create stale-by-construction risk and re-validate already-validated upstream input; NOTE preserves documentation while matching established precedent in BBAAQ Surface 4's mint vocabulary. Both lines describe runtime behavior of kindle/validate/render operations rather than retired field attribute references, so neither was hit by the docket's verification grep — left for itch capture or future structural pass. Also worth noting at heat-level: RBSDE precedent for derivation prose ('${RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX}d-${RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER} (resolved by RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID)') is now established across RBSDE, RBSGM, plus the seven specs BBAAV swept; RBSRR is the source-of-truth definition site reflecting that vocabulary.

content.text:
Notched ₢BBAAY at `07730eba`.

## Summary

All five docket surfaces landed:
- **RBSRR**: deleted three retired `axhrv_variable` blocks; added `axhrgv_variable` for `rbrr_depot_moniker` between cloud_prefix and runtime_prefix; updated group intro for "all three / two prefixes"; rewrote line 8 prose to tuple-based identity ("fall out as RBDC constants" matching paddock vocabulary)
- **RBS0**: added `:rbrr_depot_moniker:` mapping at line 327
- **RBSGM:27**: fixed in this pace — replaced stale `rbw-*-[0-9]{12}` require with NOTE per RBSDE:38 precedent ("enforced upstream by `zrbrr_enforce`")

**Itch candidates surfaced** (out of strict docket scope, not touched):
- RBSRR line 175 — `axhro_validate` prose says "Checks required fields: DEPOT_PROJECT_ID, GCP_REGION, GAR_REPOSITORY"; actual validation in rbrr_regime.sh now centers on DEPOT_MONIKER + CLOUD_PREFIX + joint-length cap
- RBSRR line 183 — `axhro_render` enumerates section names ("Artifact Registry", "Service Account Credentials", "Container Runtime Configuration") that don't match current `buv_group_enroll` calls in rbrr_regime.sh ("GCP Infrastructure", "Google Cloud Build Configuration")

Both lines describe operational behavior, not retired attribute references, so neither was hit by docket verification greps. Worth itch capture or a follow-on structural pass.

Ready to wrap ₢BBAAY?

07730eba jjb:1011-3a4ae2a5:₢BBAAY:n: Land BBAAQ Surface 4's depot-identity-collapse retirement at RBSRR (the regime spec defining rbrr.env). Carved out of BBAAV at mount time when the scope contradiction surfaced — BBAAV's docket said 'no behavioral or structural changes to spec content beyond xref flipping', but RBSRR's references to the three retired RBRR fields are definition sites, not xrefs. RBSRR-RegimeRepo.adoc: deleted three axhrv_variable definition blocks for the retired fields ({rbrr_depot_project_id}, {rbrr_gar_repository}, {rbrr_gcb_pool_stem}); added one axhrgv_variable block for the new {rbrr_depot_moniker} field (placed in the Resource Prefixing group between {rbrr_cloud_prefix} and {rbrr_runtime_prefix} so the depot identity tuple lives together — followed file's group convention axhrgv over docket's literal axhrv since cloud_prefix uses axhrgv); updated group intro at lines 13-16 from 'Operator-chosen prefixes' / 'Both variables must be populated' / 'The prefix must be 2-11 characters' to 'Operator-chosen identifiers' / 'All three variables must be populated' / 'The two prefixes must be 2-11 characters' since the moniker has a different format (^[a-z][a-z0-9]*$, length 1-26) than the prefix format (^[a-z][a-z0-9-]*-$); updated intro prose at line 8 from 'depot project identifiers, regional settings, repository locations, and service account credential file paths' to 'depot identity tuple (RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX, RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER) (from which depot project ID, GAR repository, pool stem, and bucket fall out as RBDC constants), regional settings, and service account credential file paths' — phrasing 'fall out as RBDC constants' matches paddock vocabulary at BB. Variable description for depot_moniker grounded in RBSDE-depot_levy.adoc precedent (line 31): naming 'Stem identifier that combines with {rbrr_cloud_prefix} to form the depot identity tuple', followed by format constraint and 'The tuple derives RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID, RBDC_GAR_REPOSITORY, RBDC_GCB_POOL_STEM, and RBDC_GCS_BUCKET at kindle time'. RBS0-SpecTop.adoc: added :rbrr_depot_moniker: <<rbrr_depot_moniker,RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER>> mapping at line 327, between rbrr_cloud_prefix and rbrr_runtime_prefix to preserve adjacency. Confirmed retired RBRR attributes (rbrr_depot_project_id, rbrr_gar_repository, rbrr_gcb_pool_stem) already absent from RBS0 mappings — BBAAQ Surface 4 had previously scrubbed them. RBSGM-governor_mantle.adoc:27 fix-or-itch decision (docket explicit: 'Decide: fix in this pace (small, local) or itch'): fixed in this pace. The require step '«DEPOT_PROJECT_ID» matches pattern rbw-*-[0-9]{12}' references the OLD timestamped project-ID shape from before BBAAM's depot-identity-collapse — under the new ${CLOUD_PREFIX}d-${MONIKER} shape (where CLOUD_PREFIX is ^[a-z][a-z0-9-]*-$ and MONIKER is ^[a-z][a-z0-9]*$, length 1-26), the rbw-*-[0-9]{12} pattern can never match. Replaced lines 26-27 with a NOTE following RBSDE-depot_levy.adoc:38 precedent ('Joint length ... is enforced upstream by zrbrr_enforce at regime kindle time, so this step performs no length validation') — replacement reads 'NOTE: Format and joint length of ${RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX} + d- + ${RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER} are enforced upstream by zrbrr_enforce at regime kindle time, so this step performs no shape validation'. Choice rationale: deleting outright would remove documentation value; updating regex to new shape would re-create stale-by-construction risk and re-validate already-validated upstream input; NOTE preserves documentation while matching established precedent in BBAAQ Surface 4's mint vocabulary. Verifications all green: grep for rbrr_depot_project_id|rbrr_gar_repository|rbrr_gcb_pool_stem in RBSRR returns zero hits; grep for rbrr_depot_moniker in RBS0 returns line 327 with new mapping; grep for rbw-*-[0-9]{12} in RBSGM returns zero hits. AsciiDoc render check requested in docket but asciidoctor not installed locally; textual scan of new variable block shows only valid linked-term references ({rbrr_cloud_prefix}, no unresolved attributes). Out-of-scope items surfaced for itch consideration during structural review of RBSRR (NOT touched per strict docket scope discipline): line 175 axhro_validate prose still says 'Checks required fields: DEPOT_PROJECT_ID, GCP_REGION, GAR_REPOSITORY' — actual validation per rbrr_regime.sh:50/94/97 now centers on RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER, RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX, joint-length cap (rbrr_regime.sh:103-109), and other operational fields; line 183 axhro_render prose enumerates section names 'GCP Infrastructure, Artifact Registry, Service Account Credentials, Container Runtime Configuration, Build Configuration' — actual render groups per rbrr_regime.sh:49/53 are 'GCP Infrastructure' and 'Google Cloud Build Configuration', layout has been reorganized. Both lines describe runtime behavior of kindle/validate/render operations rather than retired field attribute references, so neither was hit by the docket's verification grep — left for itch capture or future structural pass. Also worth noting at heat-level: RBSDE precedent for derivation prose ('${RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX}d-${RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER} (resolved by RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID)') is now established across RBSDE, RBSGM, plus the seven specs BBAAV swept; RBSRR is the source-of-truth definition site reflecting that vocabulary.

rbrr_render() {
  buc_doc_brief "Display diagnostic view of RBRR repo regime configuration"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  buv_render RBRR "RBRR - Recipe Bottle Regime Repo"
}

######################################################################
# Furnish and Main

zrbrr_furnish() {
  buc_doc_env "BURD_BUK_DIR          " "BUK module directory (dispatch-provided)"
  buc_doc_env "BURD_TOOLS_DIR        " "Project tools root directory (dispatch-provided)"
  buc_doc_env_done || return 0

  source "${BURD_CONFIG_DIR}/rbbc_constants.sh"
  local z_rbk_kit_dir="${BURD_TOOLS_DIR}/${RBBC_kit_subdir}"

  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buv_validation.sh"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/burd_regime.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbcc_Constants.sh"

# Walks enrollment rolls grouped by group, applying group-level gates.

buv_render() {

  local z_label="${2:-}"
  test -n "${z_scope}" || buc_die "buv_render: scope required"

message.content: I am uncomfortable because I see code appearing in the specification.  Specification language should be the core idea, and not mentions of specific bash routines.  Only code reference used to be replacement text that matched the bash function names.  Thoughts?

content.text:
You're right and the discomfort is well-founded. The discipline is: prose describes the operation's core idea, and the linked-term machinery (`{rbkro_kindle}` etc.) is the controlled binding point that resolves to the function name when rendered. Anything else — `buv_lock`, `buv_scope_sentinel`, `ZRBRR_DOCKER_ENV`, `buv_render RBRR` — is implementation seepage that ages the spec to whatever week the helper API was written.

Worse: my `fc815a27` notch *increased* the leakage. The original pre-BBAAM prose had its own seepage (`compgen`, `ZRBRR_ROLLUP`, `VAR="${VAR:-}"` pattern) — but a stale leak doesn't justify a fresh one, and I should have rewritten upward to concept language, not sideways to current-implementation bash names.

The other miss: where field names *do* belong in spec prose, they should arrive via linked terms (`{rbrr_secrets_dir}`, `{rbrr_cloud_prefix}`) to match the prevailing pattern in the rest of the file — not as bare `RBRR_SECRETS_DIR` literals.

## Proposed rewrite

**axhro_kindle:**
> Declares the RBRR fields and their types, organized by group.
> Detects unexpected RBRR_-prefixed variables, surfacing operator typos and stale configuration.
> Freezes the regime against further mutation.

**axhro_validate:**
> Confirms each RBRR field satisfies its declared type and format.
> Verifies that `{rbrr_vessel_dir}` and `{rbrr_secrets_dir}` resolve to existing directories.
> Enforces format constraints on `{rbrr_cloud_prefix}` and `{rbrr_depot_moniker}` including the joint-length cap (≤30 chars for the GCP project ID limit).

**axhro_render:**
> Produces a diagnostic display of the kindled regime by group.
> Sections: Resource Prefixing, Vessel and Local Configuration, GCP Infrastructure, Google Cloud Build Configuration, Secrets Directory, Public Docs.

Notes on what's kept and why:
- **Section name list (axhro_render)** — these are operator-visible output strings, not implementation internals. Like a UX commitment. Defensible to keep.
- **30-char GCP project ID cap** — that's a GCP rule, conceptual constraint. Defensible.
- **Linked terms for field names** — matches the rest of RBSRR.

Path forward — amend (third notch on top of `fc815a27` with the cleaner concept prose) or revert `fc815a27` first and re-land clean? I'd lean amend since the additive forward-fix preserves the trail of the conversation that produced the discipline. Your call.

## Paces

### cult-verb-foundation (₢BBAAN) [rough]

## Character

Foundation pace for the cult-verb refactor of the SA-management surface. Lands before any consumer touches the new surface, so the downstream paces (₢BBAAQ spec, ₢BBAAR handbook, ₢BBAAK theurge) start from a settled foundation.

Coding-only pace. Spec/README/handbook narrative surfaces are owned by siblings.

Intent-only docket. Code-level planning happens at mount time, in real-time conversation against current state.

## Scope

The SA-management interface refactors along three load-bearing axes.

**Cult-verb collapse for operational SAs (retriever, director).** Invest replaces charter and knight; divest replaces forfeit for operational SAs; **roster** replaces list (renamed from "muster" per paddock cult-verb naming decision — JJ owns "muster" as a verb). Per-role split: each cult verb has retriever and director instances. Mantle (governor) stays — singleton-per-depot is asymmetric with retriever/director and the existing verb captures it honestly.

**Producer-output discipline.** SA creation writes RBRA bytes to the assay slot exclusively. The `${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}/<instance>.rbra` credential write retires. Assay becomes the binding producer output for credential bytes; downstream consumers (test, operator) copy from assay to role production path.

**Observation-fact convention.** Roster emits per-row facts using `<basename>.<type>` type-as-extension naming via `buf_write_fact_multi` (₢BBAAO foundation). Stdout-substring assertions retire from the consumer-facing surface.

Divest-by-role recovers email from the role's installed RBRA via REST DELETE. No gcloud (RBSK rule). Governor SA cleanup folds into depot-unmake; no standalone demantle verb.

## Settled decisions

**Cult-verb triad.** invest, divest, roster.

**Colophon scheme.** `rbw-a[rd][IDr]` — six colophons replace four (aC, aK, aL, aF). Mantle's `rbw-aM` unchanged.

| | Retriever | Director |
|---|---|---|
| Invest | `rbw-arI` | `rbw-adI` |
| Divest | `rbw-arD` | `rbw-adD` |
| Roster | `rbw-arr` | `rbw-adr` |

Letter case carries semantic load: capitals (I, D) for cloud-mutating verbs; lowercase (r) for no-cloud-change observation. Matches existing `rbw-d[lL]` precedent (lowercase `dl` for list, uppercase `dL` for levy).

**Per-role split.** Roster, invest, and divest each have retriever and director instances. No unified surface. Six functions, six tabtargets, six RBZ enrollments.

**Mantle unchanged.** `rbw-aM` and `RBZ_MANTLE_GOVERNOR` stay. Governor cleanup folds into depot-unmake; no demantle verb.

**Singleton-per-role invest.** Zero instance arg from operator. SA name derived from role + RBRR-derived value (regime-kindled, not operator-supplied).

**Producer-output.** Invest writes RBRA to assay slot exclusively. `${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}` credential-write retires.

**Divest-by-role.** Zero-arg. Reads role's installed RBRA, extracts email, REST DELETE.

**RBYC / RBZ constants.** This pace emits the new vocabulary constants (`RBYC_INVEST` / `RBYC_DIVEST` / `RBYC_ROSTER`, `RBZ_*_RETRIEVER` / `RBZ_*_DIRECTOR` for each verb). ₢BBAAR consumes them downstream.

## Implementation conventions

**Function naming.** `rbgg_<verb>_<role>` shape: `rbgg_invest_retriever`, `rbgg_invest_director`, `rbgg_divest_retriever`, `rbgg_divest_director`, `rbgg_roster_retrievers`, `rbgg_roster_directors`. Matches existing `rbgg_charter_retriever` precedent. (The image-domain `rbfl_*` precedent — formerly `rbfl_muster_*`, renamed to `rbfl_audit_*` per paddock cult-verb decision — is a sibling convention in a separate domain.)

**Fact-extension constants.** All fact-extension tinder constants live in RBCC as one contiguous block. Migrate the existing `RBGP_FACT_EXT_DEPOT` from RBGC into this block; new retriever and director fact-extension constants join it. Exact constant names follow RBCC's tinder convention at mount.

**Roster fact content.** Empty (presence-as-the-fact, matches `BUF_EXT_ALIAS` precedent). Promote later if a consumer surfaces a need.

**Divest post-delete cleanup.** After successful cloud DELETE, divest removes the role's production RBRA file. Mirrors cloud state; prevents stale-credential reuse.

**Invest preexistence posture.** Fatal if the SA already exists in cloud. Operator divests first to re-key. Matches BBAAM's "fails if exists" levy posture.

**Unmake governor-absent posture.** Depot-unmake's folded governor cleanup tolerates 404 via the existing `rbgu_http_require_ok "..." "..." 404 "not found"` pattern (already used by `rbgg_forfeit_service_account`). No special detection or fail-fast — DELETE returns 200 or 404, both fine; depot teardown proceeds.

The mapping is asymmetric: knight specs map 1:1 to invest specs (clean
ancestry preservation), but sa_forfeit and sa_list each split per-role
(ancestry preserved on one side of each split, second side authored
fresh).

| Source orphan | Target file (via `git mv`) | Ancestry-preserved? | Fresh-authored sibling |
|---------------|----------------------------|---------------------|------------------------|
| RBSDK-director_knight.adoc | RBSDK-director_invest.adoc | yes | — |

## Two-stage commit strategy (git-mv ancestry)

Each target file lands in two commits to keep git's rename detection
robust against wholesale content replacement:

**Stage 1 — pure rename, content unchanged:**
```
git mv RBSDK-director_knight.adoc RBSDK-director_invest.adoc
git mv RBSRK-retriever_knight.adoc RBSRK-retriever_invest.adoc
git mv RBSSF-sa_forfeit.adoc RBSDD-director_divest.adoc
git mv RBSSL-sa_list.adoc RBSDR-director_roster.adoc
```
Single commit captures all four renames. `git log --follow` will trace
each new path back through the rename to the orphan's full history.

**Stage 2 — content rewrite per-file:**
Each renamed file gets its content replaced to match the new
behavioral contract (see "Behavioral-contract deltas" below). Per-file
commits keep diffs reviewable. Content replacement is wholesale — old
knight/charter/forfeit/list semantics fully gone — but the file path
through which history is traced remains intact.

**Stage 3 — author fresh siblings:**
RBSRD-retriever_divest.adoc and RBSRL-retriever_roster.adoc are written
new. Content shape mirrors the director-side sibling (which got the
ancestry), with role-specific adjustments. These two have no git
ancestry by necessity — one source can't ancestry-bear two targets.

## Paces

### cult-verb-foundation (₢BBAAN) [rough]

## Character

Foundation pace for the cult-verb refactor of the SA-management surface. Lands before any consumer touches the new surface, so the downstream paces (₢BBAAQ spec, ₢BBAAR handbook, ₢BBAAK theurge) start from a settled foundation.

Coding-only pace. Spec/README/handbook narrative surfaces are owned by siblings.

Intent-only docket. Code-level planning happens at mount time, in real-time conversation against current state.

## Scope

The SA-management interface refactors along three load-bearing axes.

**Cult-verb collapse for operational SAs (retriever, director).** Invest replaces charter and knight; divest replaces forfeit for operational SAs; **roster** replaces list (renamed from "muster" per paddock cult-verb naming decision — JJ owns "muster" as a verb). Per-role split: each cult verb has retriever and director instances. Mantle (governor) stays — singleton-per-depot is asymmetric with retriever/director and the existing verb captures it honestly.

**Producer-output discipline.** SA creation writes RBRA bytes to the assay slot exclusively. The `${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}/<instance>.rbra` credential write retires. Assay becomes the binding producer output for credential bytes; downstream consumers (test, operator) copy from assay to role production path.

**Observation-fact convention.** Roster emits per-row facts using `<basename>.<type>` type-as-extension naming via `buf_write_fact_multi` (₢BBAAO foundation). Stdout-substring assertions retire from the consumer-facing surface.

Divest-by-role recovers email from the role's installed RBRA via REST DELETE. No gcloud (RBSK rule). Governor SA cleanup folds into depot-unmake; no standalone demantle verb.

## Settled decisions

**Cult-verb triad.** invest, divest, roster.

**Colophon scheme.** `rbw-a[rd][IDr]` — six colophons replace four (aC, aK, aL, aF). Mantle's `rbw-aM` unchanged.

| | Retriever | Director |
|---|---|---|
| Invest | `rbw-arI` | `rbw-adI` |
| Divest | `rbw-arD` | `rbw-adD` |
| Roster | `rbw-arr` | `rbw-adr` |

Letter case carries semantic load: capitals (I, D) for cloud-mutating verbs; lowercase (r) for no-cloud-change observation. Matches existing `rbw-d[lL]` precedent (lowercase `dl` for list, uppercase `dL` for levy).

**Per-role split.** Roster, invest, and divest each have retriever and director instances. No unified surface. Six functions, six tabtargets, six RBZ enrollments.

**Mantle unchanged.** `rbw-aM` and `RBZ_MANTLE_GOVERNOR` stay. Governor cleanup folds into depot-unmake; no demantle verb.

**Singleton-per-role invest.** Zero instance arg from operator. SA name derived from role + RBRR-derived value (regime-kindled, not operator-supplied).

**Producer-output.** Invest writes RBRA to assay slot exclusively. `${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}` credential-write retires.

**Divest-by-role.** Zero-arg. Reads role's installed RBRA, extracts email, REST DELETE.

**RBYC / RBZ constants.** This pace emits the new vocabulary constants (`RBYC_INVEST` / `RBYC_DIVEST` / `RBYC_ROSTER`, `RBZ_*_RETRIEVER` / `RBZ_*_DIRECTOR` for each verb). ₢BBAAR consumes them downstream.

## Implementation conventions

**Function naming.** `rbgg_<verb>_<role>` shape: `rbgg_invest_retriever`, `rbgg_invest_director`, `rbgg_divest_retriever`, `rbgg_divest_director`, `rbgg_roster_retrievers`, `rbgg_roster_directors`. Matches existing `rbgg_charter_retriever` precedent. (The image-domain `rbfl_*` precedent — formerly `rbfl_muster_*`, renamed to `rbfl_audit_*` per paddock cult-verb decision — is a sibling convention in a separate domain.)

**Fact-extension constants.** All fact-extension tinder constants live in RBCC as one contiguous block. Migrate the existing `RBGP_FACT_EXT_DEPOT` from RBGC into this block; new retriever and director fact-extension constants join it. Exact constant names follow RBCC's tinder convention at mount.

**Roster fact content.** Empty (presence-as-the-fact, matches `BUF_EXT_ALIAS` precedent). Promote later if a consumer surfaces a need.

**Divest post-delete cleanup.** After successful cloud DELETE, divest removes the role's production RBRA file. Mirrors cloud state; prevents stale-credential reuse.

**Invest preexistence posture.** Fatal if the SA already exists in cloud. Operator divests first to re-key. Matches BBAAM's "fails if exists" levy posture.

**Unmake governor-absent posture.** Depot-unmake's folded governor cleanup tolerates 404 via the existing `rbgu_http_require_ok "..." "..." 404 "not found"` pattern (already used by `rbgg_forfeit_service_account`). No special detection or fail-fast — DELETE returns 200 or 404, both fine; depot teardown proceeds.

## Cross-pace dependencies

- **₢BBAAO** (multi-fact foundation, landed): consumed for roster's per-row fact emission via `buf_write_fact_multi`.
- **₢BBAAQ** (spec sweep): consumes settled vocabulary; runs after this pace.
- **₢BBAAR** (handbook sweep): consumes settled `RBYC_*` / `RBZ_*` constant renames and verb-as-noun ceremony pattern (`(Invest):`, `(Divest):`, `(Roster):`); runs after this pace.
- **₢BBAAK** (theurge consumer): consumes new colophons, the per-role fact-file convention, and the assay-as-binding-output flow; runs after this pace.

## Constraints

- Honor paddock's Release-branch execution contract.
- Real-time review at each step before commit.
- No pre-staged step enumeration.

## Out of scope

- Spec/README quoin authoring (₢BBAAQ).
- Operator-handbook narrative sweep (₢BBAAR).
- Theurge consumer adaptation (₢BBAAK).
- Multi-instance RBRA path scheme (singleton-per-role path stands).
- Mantle ceremony or governor lifecycle change beyond fold-into-unmake.

## Two-stage commit strategy (git-mv ancestry)

Each target file lands in two commits to keep git's rename detection
robust against wholesale content replacement:

**Stage 1 — pure rename, content unchanged:**
```
git mv RBSDK-director_knight.adoc RBSDK-director_invest.adoc
git mv RBSRK-retriever_knight.adoc RBSRK-retriever_invest.adoc
git mv RBSSF-sa_forfeit.adoc RBSDD-director_divest.adoc
git mv RBSSL-sa_list.adoc RBSDR-director_roster.adoc
```
Single commit captures all four renames. `git log --follow` will trace
each new path back through the rename to the orphan's full history.

**Stage 2 — content rewrite per-file:**
Each renamed file gets its content replaced to match the new
behavioral contract (see "Behavioral-contract deltas" below). Per-file
commits keep diffs reviewable. Content replacement is wholesale — old
knight/charter/forfeit/list semantics fully gone — but the file path
through which history is traced remains intact.

**Stage 3 — author fresh siblings:**
RBSRD-retriever_divest.adoc and RBSRL-retriever_roster.adoc are written
new. Content shape mirrors the director-side sibling (which got the
ancestry), with role-specific adjustments. These two have no git
ancestry by necessity — one source can't ancestry-bear two targets.

## Behavioral-contract deltas (from RBS0:1045-1119)

Each new spec must reflect these BBAAQ Surface 2 contracts, which differ
from the orphans' originals:

**invest** (was knight/charter):
- Singleton-per-role: zero instance argument from operator
- SA name derived from role + depot moniker (regime-kindled), not operator-supplied
- Producer-output discipline: writes RBRA bytes to the assay slot exclusively;
  the `${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}` credential write retires
- Fatal if SA already exists; operator divests first to re-key

**divest** (was sa_forfeit, now per-role):
- Zero-arg by-role lookup: reads role's installed RBRA, extracts SA email,
  issues REST DELETE
- After successful cloud DELETE, removes role's production RBRA file to
  mirror cloud state and prevent stale-credential reuse

**roster** (was sa_list, now per-role):
- Read-only observation
- Emits one fact file per discovered SA via BUS0 multi-fact convention
  (`buf_write_fact_multi` against a registered extension constant); fact
  content is empty, presence-as-fact
- Stdout-substring assertions retire from consumer-facing surface

Pristine-lifecycle section restructures. BBAAJ's §2 (pristine-lifecycle-simple) and BBAAK's original §3 (pristine-lifecycle-credentialed) collapse into a single unified section containing three fused cases that share one throwaway depot for the run:

## BBAAN sequencing implications

These flow from BBAAN's settled implementation conventions and constrain the fused-case ordering.

**Post-divest production RBRA absence.** "Divests of operational SAs" leaves no production RBRA at the role's path (BBAAN's divest-deletes-production-RBRA decision). The current SA-cycle ordering (mantle → invest → access-probes → divest) is correct as written; tear-down does not need credentials.

**Invest fatal-on-exists.** Re-invest within a single fixture run requires divest first (BBAAN's fatal-if-exists decision). The fused-case design already lands invest exactly once per role; no in-run re-invest needed.

**Governor cleanup folded into unmake.** No separate "forfeit governor" step appears in the fused case — `Depot tear-down — unmake, which absorbs governor cleanup` is the entire cleanup. The 404-tolerant unmake makes ordering robust to mid-fixture failures (an unmake after a failed mantle still cleans up cleanly).

**RBCC fact-extension constants.** BBAAN consolidates fact-extension tinder constants into one contiguous block in RBCC (migrating `RBGP_FACT_EXT_DEPOT` from RBGC; new retriever/director ext constants join it). Rust-side consumers — including `RBTDRP_FACT_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID` and any new fact-name imports — must point to the RBCC home. ## Character

Restorative spec authoring. BBAAQ Surface 2 inlined six cult-verb operations
into RBS0 (lines 1045-1119) as 5-7 line abstract behavioral contracts and
left the original procedural sub-spec files (RBSDK, RBSRK, RBSSF, RBSSL)
un-included on disk. The orphans hold detail the new RBS0 inline content
does not: API endpoint URLs, HTTP response code expectations,
`axhopa_arg_parameter` / `axhos_step` / `axhoot_typed_output` /
`axhoc_completion` blocks, liturgical links to Google REST docs.

This pace re-authors the procedural specifications so the new cult-verb
operations sit on the same depth tier as RBSDE / RBSDU / RBSGM / RBSDV /
RBSDY (full procedural sub-specs included by RBS0), AND preserves git
ancestry of the orphans by using `git mv` rather than create-then-delete.
The orphans are still on disk un-deleted, so the rename path is open;
preserving blame/log on the procedural detail is a load-bearing recovery
of project history.

Pure .adoc — no code, no narrative tracks — but with judgment work on
each behavioral-contract adaptation.

Runs after BBAAY (regime-spec restructure) so the depot-identity rewrite
landing pattern is settled when the new procedural specs reference RBRR
fields.

## Source orphans → target operations (with ancestry strategy)

The mapping is asymmetric: knight specs map 1:1 to invest specs (clean
ancestry preservation), but sa_forfeit and sa_list each split per-role
(ancestry preserved on one side of each split, second side authored
fresh).

| Source orphan | Target file (via `git mv`) | Ancestry-preserved? | Fresh-authored sibling |
|---------------|----------------------------|---------------------|------------------------|
| RBSDK-director_knight.adoc | RBSDK-director_invest.adoc | yes | — |
| RBSRK-retriever_knight.adoc | RBSRK-retriever_invest.adoc | yes | — |
| RBSSF-sa_forfeit.adoc | RBSDD-director_divest.adoc | yes (director side) | RBSRD-retriever_divest.adoc (fresh) |
| RBSSL-sa_list.adoc | RBSDR-director_roster.adoc | yes (director side) | RBSRL-retriever_roster.adoc (fresh) |

Acronym suggestions are subject to executor verification against terminal
exclusivity (CLAUDE.md prefix discipline) — RBSDD / RBSRD / RBSDR / RBSRL
are candidate mints; if any collide, executor picks alternates and
documents the choice.

Director-side claims the rename for splits because:
- D < R alphabetically gives a stable convention
- Equivalent choice; only constraint is consistency across the two splits

## Two-stage commit strategy (git-mv ancestry)

Each target file lands in two commits to keep git's rename detection
robust against wholesale content replacement:

**Stage 1 — pure rename, content unchanged:**
```
git mv RBSDK-director_knight.adoc RBSDK-director_invest.adoc
git mv RBSRK-retriever_knight.adoc RBSRK-retriever_invest.adoc
git mv RBSSF-sa_forfeit.adoc RBSDD-director_divest.adoc
git mv RBSSL-sa_list.adoc RBSDR-director_roster.adoc
```
Single commit captures all four renames. `git log --follow` will trace
each new path back through the rename to the orphan's full history.

**Stage 2 — content rewrite per-file:**
Each renamed file gets its content replaced to match the new
behavioral contract (see "Behavioral-contract deltas" below). Per-file
commits keep diffs reviewable. Content replacement is wholesale — old
knight/charter/forfeit/list semantics fully gone — but the file path
through which history is traced remains intact.

**Stage 3 — author fresh siblings:**
RBSRD-retriever_divest.adoc and RBSRL-retriever_roster.adoc are written
new. Content shape mirrors the director-side sibling (which got the
ancestry), with role-specific adjustments. These two have no git
ancestry by necessity — one source can't ancestry-bear two targets.

## Behavioral-contract deltas (from RBS0:1045-1119)

Each new spec must reflect these BBAAQ Surface 2 contracts, which differ
from the orphans' originals:

**invest** (was knight/charter):
- Singleton-per-role: zero instance argument from operator
- SA name derived from role + depot moniker (regime-kindled), not operator-supplied
- Producer-output discipline: writes RBRA bytes to the assay slot exclusively;
  the `${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}` credential write retires
- Fatal if SA already exists; operator divests first to re-key

**divest** (was sa_forfeit, now per-role):
- Zero-arg by-role lookup: reads role's installed RBRA, extracts SA email,
  issues REST DELETE
- After successful cloud DELETE, removes role's production RBRA file to
  mirror cloud state and prevent stale-credential reuse

**roster** (was sa_list, now per-role):
- Read-only observation
- Emits one fact file per discovered SA via BUS0 multi-fact convention
  (`buf_write_fact_multi` against a registered extension constant); fact
  content is empty, presence-as-fact
- Stdout-substring assertions retire from consumer-facing surface

## Procedural detail to preserve (from orphans)

Each new spec carries forward the orphans' shape:

- `//axhob_operation` opener with operation quoin
- NOTE block describing the operation's role and scope
- `//axhopa_arg_parameter` / `//axhopt_typed_parameter` blocks (or omit
  parameters where the new contract is zero-arg)
- `//axhos_step` blocks with `{rbbc_*}` action verbs (`{rbbc_call}`,
  `{rbbc_require}`, `{rbbc_store}`, `{rbbc_fatal}`, `{rbbc_show}`)
- API endpoint URLs (link form: `link:https://cloud.google.com/iam/...[...]`)
- HTTP response code expectations (200 / 404 handling, etc.)
- `//axhoot_typed_output` block declaring output type
- `//axhoc_completion` closing prose

Match the depth and shape of RBSDE-depot_levy.adoc as the gold standard
for a procedural sub-spec.

## RBS0 restructure

Once the six new sub-spec files exist:

- Anchors `[[rbtgo_director_invest]]` etc. move from RBS0 inline content
  (currently lines 1045-1119) into the new sub-spec files (each file's
  `axhob_operation` opener carries the anchor).
- RBS0 lines 1045-1119 are replaced by `include::RBSDK-director_invest.adoc[]`
  / etc. directives, plus any cross-cutting prose that was inline (the
  preamble at 1039-1043 about cult-verb triad and colophon shape may stay
  inline as cross-cutting framing).
- Mapping section unchanged (the six attribute references already exist
  at RBS0:117-132).

## Orphan disposal

Replaced by ancestry-preserving rename strategy above. No `git rm`
required — the orphans transform into the new files via `git mv` (four
of the six) plus two fresh siblings. CLAUDE.md File Acronym Mappings
section updates to reflect:
- RBSDK frontispiece changes (knight → invest)
- RBSRK frontispiece changes (knight → invest)
- RBSSF and RBSSL entries removed (renamed away)
- New entries added for RBSDD, RBSRD, RBSDR, RBSRL (or alternates if
  executor mints differently)

## Companion sweep: RBSRR axho_* prose discipline

RBSRR's three operations paragraphs (`axhro_kindle` / `axhro_validate` /
`axhro_render`) currently leak BUK helper names (`buv_*`, `ZRBRR_*`)
into prose — surfaced during BBAAY notch `fc815a27` review (the followup
landed on top of the original docket-scoped `07730eba` and over-corrected
stale field references into bash-routine references). Sweep with the same
concept-language + linked-term discipline being applied to the new
procedural specs above. Field references via linked terms
(`{rbrr_vessel_dir}` etc.), not bare uppercase. The 30-char GCP project
ID joint-length cap and the operator-visible section-name list
enumerated by `axhro_render` (Resource Prefixing, Vessel and Local
Configuration, GCP Infrastructure, Google Cloud Build Configuration,
Secrets Directory, Public Docs) stay — they describe operator-visible
output, not implementation.

## Verification

- Six new sub-spec files exist with full procedural detail (axho* blocks,
  API endpoints, HTTP codes).
- `git log --follow` on each of the four ancestry-preserved files shows
  history reaching back through the rename to the orphan's full log.

- AsciiDoc rendering of RBS0 shows the new operations at the same depth

- `grep -n 'rbtgo_director_invest\|rbtgo_director_divest\|rbtgo_director_roster\|rbtgo_retriever_invest\|rbtgo_retriever_divest\|rbtgo_retriever_roster'`
  shows anchors live in sub-spec files; mapping references in RBS0; xref
  consumers (RBSGS post-BBAAV) resolve correctly.
- `grep -E 'buv_|ZRBRR_' Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/RBSRR-RegimeRepo.adoc`
  returns zero hits (companion-sweep success).
- CLAUDE.md File Acronym Mappings section updated.

## Out of scope

- Code changes to rbgg_Governor.sh, rbgi_IAM.sh, or any bash module
- Behavioral changes to operations beyond the BBAAQ Surface 2 contracts
- Changes to other operations' specs (RBSDE, RBSDU, RBSGM, etc.)
- Tabtarget files
- Test fixtures

## References

- BBAAQ commit b73ae194 (wrap) — Surface 2 commit 202c7726 inlined the
  abstracts; orphan disposal explicitly framed as deferred to BBAAN.
- BBAAV commit 021aff0f — companion mechanical xref sweep; surfaced the
  RBS0:1045-1108 inline content for operator review.
- BBAAY notch fc815a27 — RBSRR axho_* paragraphs landed bash-routine

- RBSDE-depot_levy.adoc — gold-standard procedural sub-spec shape.
